key_points: Marginalisation means being pushed to the edge of society and having less access to resources and opportunities
Groups such as Adivasis, Dalits and minorities have faced marginalisation in India
Marginalised communities often experience discrimination and social inequality
The Constitution provides Fundamental Rights to protect these communities
The government has introduced laws and policies to support marginalised groups
Protective measures help ensure equality and justice in society
examples: The Constitution abolishes untouchability to protect Dalits
Adivasi communities are given special protections for their land and culture
Reservation policies help marginalised groups get better access to education and jobs
Courts can take action if discrimination against these groups occurs
formulas: Marginalisation → Social exclusion + Lack of opportunities
Social justice → Protection of vulnerable groups
exam_tips: Mention examples like Adivasis and Dalits
Explain how the Constitution protects marginalised groups
Use terms such as equality, rights and social justice in answers
